issues: Generic placeholder images for project cards significantly detract from the portfolio's authenticity and polish.
Lack of visible interactivity cues (e.g., hover states on cards, active navigation indicators) makes the site feel static.
Small text elements (e.g., 'Portfolio 2025', footer links) are too small, impacting readability.
Generic placeholder images in the project section detract from authenticity.
Lack of subtle depth and polish elements (e.g., more pronounced shadows, subtle gradients, glassmorphism) makes some sections feel flat.
strengths: Effective use of bold, distinctive typography that creates a modern and professional feel.
Overall visual hierarchy is clear, guiding the user through the content effectively.
Clear visual hierarchy effectively guides the user through main content sections.
Cohesive and professional dark color system with effective, restrained use of the accent color.
Strong and distinctive typography for headings creates a bold visual identity.
specific_improvements: Introduce more dynamic or asymmetric layout elements, especially in the project gallery or about section, to enhance visual interest.
Introduce more refined depth to cards (e.g., subtle shadows, gradient borders, or slight background variations) to enhance the overall craftsmanship.
Replace generic project images with actual project screenshots/mockups and add interactive hover states.
Implement subtle hover effects for all interactive elements, especially project and skill cards, to indicate clickability and add a layer of polish.
Replace generic project images with diverse, high-quality project thumbnails that showcase actual work and include hover effects for interactivity.
